SubjectInfoText:
The GSB does not record the pedigree of the Bustler Mare, traditionally foundation mare of Family 8. However, Cuthbert Routh in his stud book (1716-1752) states that his horse, [Routh's] Surley (1720), was purchased from Mr. Hutton in April of 1725, at that time recording the colt's pedigree: "gott by [Hutton's] Surley, which Horse was gott by his Gray Barb, his dam by ye Royall Colt, her dam by Byrley Turk, her dam by Bustler, her dam by a Barb, and her dam by Bay Dodsworth.
This extends the pedigree of the Bustler Mare back two further generations, the known Foundation Mare of Family 8 therefore being the mare by Bay Dodsworth (probably the same horse as Dodsworth), the dam of this Barb Mare
The second earliest mare in the lineage of Family 8
Dam of an unnamed mare by Bustler, through which the maternal line of Family 8 continues

Owner: 1) Charles Paulet, 2nd Duke of Bolton 2) Charles Paulet, 3rd Duke of Bolton
SubjectInfoText:
Owner: 1) Charles Paulet, 2nd Duke of Bolton 2) Charles Paulet, 3rd Duke of Bolton
Breeder: Sir Matthew Pierson
7 August 1710 - aged only 5yo won [running as Brown Lusty] Gold Cup for 6yo in three 4 mile heats at York
Won Great Subscription Purse Middleham Moor; a rich Prize at Quainton Meadow; match versus Wyndham (3 Nov 1712); match versus Merlin; two matches versus Dragon
Sire of 1) [Goldolphin] Whitefoot, winner of 5 matches at Newmarket 1724-1728 and a successful sire; 2) the grandam of Marske, sire of the great racehorse and sire Eclipse; 3) Bonny Lass, grandam of Spectator (1756 Jockey Club Plate), sire of Mark Anthony (sire of Aimwell 1785 Derby), and Sister to Juno (dam of Diomed); 4) Gipsey, grandam of Snap, four times Champion Sire;
and 5) [Bolton] Starling (4 King's Plates 1733, Champion Sire 1744, sire of Starling [Ancaster] and Skim) and of Looby (2 King's Plates 1734)
Champion Sire 1724, 1726, 1727, 1729, 1732, 1733 and 1734
Originally named
Brown Lusty
Died at Bolton Hall, Bedale, Yorkshire in 1736

Owner: John Crofts
The fourth earliest mare in the lineage of Family 4
Said to be given by Queen Anne to John Crofts
Dam of an unnamed mare by Brimmer through which the maternal line of the family continues
Dam of Bay Layton (by [Darcy's] Counsellor), from which branches 4-o and 4-p of Family 4 descend; and of Brown Farewell (by Makeless), through which all other branches of Family 4 were perpetuated to the present day
descend from the other Family 12 Foundation Mare, Montague Mare
The two other additional Family 12 founders arise from misattribution of two early American matrilineages to the family of imported Diana (1754, Family 12-b)

Owner: James Darcy the younger
Breeder: Rowland Place
Bred by Rowland Place and probably owned by James Darcy the younger. Despite being said to have had a limited mare book, he sired three good stallion sons:- 1) Blunderbuss (sire of the 4th dam of the top class racehorse Black Chance, King's Plate winner Phantom and good class racehorse Moorcock; and sire of Darcy's Royal Mare, ancestress of Highflyer); 2) [Old] Merlin, winner of a famous match at Newmarket, immortalised in song, and the sire of the important broodmare sire [Bethell's] Castaway; and sire of the Foundation Mares of Families 26 and 55;
and 3) Darcy's Woodcock [probably the same horse as Davill's Woodcock], the sire of Brown Woodcock, an early mare in Family 2; Old Montague Mare (dam of outstanding racehorse Sedbury); and the Foundation Mares of Families 18 and 62
Bustler also sired a number of important mares, which are the Foundation Mares of Family 24, 35 and 44

The second earliest mare in the lineage of Family 34
Dam of an unnamed mare by [Hutton's] Coneyskins through which the maternal line of the family continues
Grandam of [Hutton's] Blacklegs, sire of The Ruby Mare, dam of Marske, sire of the great racehorse and sire Eclipse, through which the Darley Arabian direct male line was perpetuated to the present day in approximately 95% of all the thoroughbreds in the world

Owner: Sir John Parsons
Breeder: Mr. Wilkes
Sire of top class racehorse Fox (1720 King's Plates Newmarket, Lewes, 1721 King's Plate Newmarket, Champion Sire 1731 and 1735); and of Fox Cub (sire of an unnamed mare which is grandam of Marske, Champion Sire 1775 and 1776, and sire of 1) Transit (1774 Jockey Club Plate); 2) Shark (19 victories including 1775 Claret Stakes, and winner of over £16,000, a record at the time); 3) the top class racehorse Pretender; and 4) the peerless Eclipse, the greatest racehorse of the 18th century, unbeaten winner of 18 races including 12 King's Plates, second in the list of leading sires 11 times, every year from 1778 and 1788,
and progenitor of the Darley Arabian male line from which over 95% of the world's current thoroughbreds descend)
Full brother to Grey Wilkes, a significant broodmare, particularly as dam of Sister to Old Country Wench, dam of Squirt, grandsire of Eclipse

Breeder: Edward Leedes
mtDNA evidence has shown she is not related to her supposed sister Betty Percival, so Cream Cheeks cannot be a descendant of Old Bald Peg, the Foundation Mare of Family 6. Her descendants have a different haplotype from any horse so far DNA tested. The DNA evidence is supported by the stud book of Cuthbert Routh which says Cream Cheeks is out of a famous roan mare of Sr. [Sir] Mar. [Marmaduke] Wyvill's. Routh and Wyvill's studs were less than 10 miles apart so Routh's pedigree is likely to be correct.
The DNA result means that, unless Charming Jenny was the same horse as Wyvill's Roan Mare and also Betty Percival was not out of Charming Jenny, which is possible but unlikely, all of Cream Cheeks's descendants should be removed from Family 6, including Flying Childers, Bartlet's Childers, Sorcerer and Priam.

Owner: 1) William Wildman 2) Dennis O'Kelly
Breeder: Prince William, Duke of Cumberland
Winnings: 18 Starts: 18 - 0 - 0, 2,149 guineas
1769:- 3 Apr - £50 Epsom; 29 May - £50 Ascot (from Creme de Barbade); 13 Jun - King's Plate [KP] Winchester; 13 Jun -£50 Winchester (w.o.); 28 Jun - KP Salisbury (w.o.); 29 Jun - City Free Plate Salisbury (from Sulphur); 25 Jul - KP Canterbury (w.o.); 27 Jul - KP Lewes; 19 Sep - KP Lichfield; 1770:- 17 Apr - 400gns match Newmarket v Bucephalus; 19 Apr - KP Newmarket; 5 Jun - KP Guildford (w.o.); 3 Jul - KP Nottingham (w.o.); 20 Aug - KP York (w.o.); 23 Aug - Great Subscription Purse [6yo+ division] York (1/20, from Tortoise and Bellario); 3 Sep - KP Lincoln (w.o.); 3 Oct - 150gns Subscription Newmarket (1/70, from Corsican); 4 Oct - KP Newmarket (w.o.)
Considered the greatest racehorse of the 18th century, undefeated in 18 career starts
Great stallion and continued progenitor of the Darley Arabian male line from which over 95% of the world's current thoroughbreds descend
2nd in Champion Sire list 11 times 1778-1788, sire of Pot-8-Os, King Fergus, Dungannon, Saltram, Serjeant and Annette
Died 26 February 1789 of colic, aged 24

Owner: Thomas Fairfax, 3rd Baron Cameron
The Fairfax Morocco Barb is the earliest foaled stallion which has progeny listed in the General Stud Book. He stood at stud at Helmsley, Yorkshire. Thomas Fairfax, 3rd Baron Cameron, was granted the Helmsley estate by Oliver Cromwell for Fairfax's service as a parliamentary general and as a "salve for a bad wound" he suffered when besieging Helmsley Castle in 1644 in the English Civil War. There is a strong possibility that the Fairfax Morocco Barb is one of the four stallions presented in 1637 to Charles I by the Emperor of Morocco
His only known progeny is the highly influential Old Morocco Mare, bred by Thomas Fairfax at the Helmsley Stud 1655c
She is dam of Spanker, important sire and described as the best racehorse at Newmarket in the time of Charles II; Young Bald Peg (grandam of Basto and top class racer and twice Champion Sire Fox); and Charming Jenny (dam of Betty Percival). The GSB also says Charming Jenny, was the dam of Cream Creeks, but mtDNA evidence has shown that Cream Cheeks and Betty Percival are not out of the same dam

Owner: Captain Rider
The equal second earliest mare in the lineage of Family 15
Dam of Points (by St. Victor's Barb) through which the maternal line of the family continues and from which branch 15-a of the family evolves
Dam of the significant sire Bald Galloway (Champion Sire 1725); and 3rd dam of Amorett - dam of 1) Blank - Champion Sire in 1762, 1764, 1770; 2) Old England (1747 King's Plate Guildford, 1748 King's Plate Newmarket); 3) Shakespeare (1751 King's Plates Guildford and Lincoln, sire of the dam of Sweetbriar, top class racehorse undefeated in 8 starts, and of Eager - (1791 Derby));
4) Tortoise (1743 King's Plate Nottingham, 1744 King's Plate Newmarket); and 5) Trimmer (1749 King's Plate Lichfield, 1750 King's Plate Newmarket)

and grandam of [Egerton] Nanny (1729 King's Plate [mares] Hambleton, 1730 King's Plate [mares] Newmarket)

Owner: 1) Thomas Fairfax 2) George Villliers, 2nd Duke of Buckingham
SubjectInfoText:
Owner: 1) Thomas Fairfax 2) George Villliers, 2nd Duke of Buckingham
The Helmsley Turk was from the stud of Lord Fairfax, Helmsley, and became the property of George Villiers, 2nd Duke of Buckingham, who married Fairfax's daughter Mary in 1657, thus regaining the Helmsley estate (which had been owned by Villiers' father, the 1st Duke Of Buckingham until his assassination in 1628) which had been given to Fairfax by Oliver Cromwell for Fairfax's service as a parliamentary general and as "a salve for a bad wound" he suffered when besieging Helmsley Castle in 1644 in the English Civil War. Fairfax died in 1671.
The Helmsley Turk's principal contribution to the breed was 1) his son Bustler, who got the famous Bustler mares of Families 8, 35, 37 and 44 and three important sons, Blunderbuss, [Old] Merlin and [Darcy's] Woodcock; and 2) Vixen, the second earliest mare in the lineage of Family 32

Owner: John Hutton II
Breeder: John Hutton II
There were two Coneyskins in the early years of the 18th century, Rutland's Coneyskins and Hutton's Coneyskins and confusion exists between their stud records
Most or all of the progeny attributed in the General Stud Book to [Rutland's] Coneyskins were probably sired by [Hutton's] Coneyskins
Pond's Sporting Kalendar, page 166, says in pedigree for Ramper:- "his Grandam by Hutton's Coneyskin (this was not the Rutland of that name)", clearly demonstrating there were two different horses
Sire of 1) the dam of [Hutton's] Blacklegs, sire of The Ruby Mare, dam of Marske (sire of the great racehorse and sire Eclipse), and through which mare one of the maternal lines of Family 8 continues, subsequently evolving into branches 8-a, 8-e and 8-j; and of 2) another unnamed daughter which continues another line of Family 8 from which branches 8-c, 8-d, 8-g, 8-h and 8-k evolve

Owner: John Hutton II
Breeder: John Hutton II
3rd dam of Bay Ranger's dam (ancestress of the great racemare stayer Beeswing - winner of 51 of her 64 starts, including the 1842 Ascot Gold Cup, 1837, 1840, 1841 and 1842 Doncaster Cup and the Newcastle Cup six times, and dam of Nunnykirk (1849 Two Thousand Guineas Stakes) and Newminster (1851 St Leger and Champion Sire 1859 and 1863)

Owner: 1); James FitzJames, 1st Duke of Berwick 2) Matthew Lister
SubjectInfoText:
Owner: 1); James FitzJames, 1st Duke of Berwick 2) Matthew Lister
Also known as
Lister's Grey Turk
and the
Stradling Turk
Said to have been taken from the Turks at the seige of Buda in 1686 and brought to England by James FitzJames, 1st Duke of Berwick, illegitimate son of James II who was aged only 17 when returning from Buda. After being acquired by Matthew Lister, he stood at his Burwell Park stud, near Louth, in Lincolnshire
Sire of 1) Snake, a very successful and significant stallion, sire of a) [William's] Squirrel (1725 King's Plates York, Newmarket, 1726 King's Plate Newmarket and sire of Fair Helen, Foundation Mare of Family 10); b) Smith's Son of Snake [Easby Snake], sire of Mother Western, grandam of Eclipse;
and c) the dam of Squirt, sire of Marske, the sire of Eclipse, through which the Darley Arabian sire line extends to the present day in over 95% of thoroughbreds; of 2) [Rutland's] Coneyskins (1718 King's Plates York, Nottingham, Lincoln, Newmarket, 1719 King's Plate Newmarket); and of 3) a number of mares which breed on including The Hobby Mare, a significant broodmare in Family 23

Foundation Mare Family 37
The family is still extant
Dam of Sir Matthew Pierson's Sorrel Running Mare, dam of [Old] Merlin, sire of 1) [Bethell's] Castaway, sire of the dam of [Appleyard's] Conqueror (winner of 6 King's Plates); and of the dam of Bald Charlotte (winner of 3 King's Plates); 2) [Darcy's] Woodcock, sire of a) Brown Farewell, from which much of Family 2 descends; b) Grey Woodcock, dam of Badger (winner of 3 King's Plates); c) Old Montague Mare, dam of the notable racehorse and sire Sedbury (winner of 4 King's Plates); and d) Woodcock Thornton, through which Family 2-b descends; 3) 5th dam of outstanding racehorse and great sire [King] Herod (8 times Champion Sire);
4) Jester, dam of Manica, sire of King's Plates winners Stump and Aldby Jenny; and of Sister to Merlin's Dam, 4th dam of top class racehorse and 7 times Champion Sire Bay Bolton, and 4th dam of top class racehorse Lamprie
Nothing is known of the origins or location of this mare

Owner: 1) George Villiers, 2nd Duke of Buckingham 2) Thomas Fairfax 3) George Villiers, 2nd Duke of Buckingham (second time)
SubjectInfoText:
Owner: 1) George Villiers, 2nd Duke of Buckingham 2) Thomas Fairfax 3) George Villiers, 2nd Duke of Buckingham (second time)
Foundation Mare Family 6
The second earliest (to the Bolton Castle mare) thoroughbred family foundation mare, Old Bald Peg (probably foaled 1645c) is located at Helmsley, Yorkshire, indicating she may be a descendant of mares belonging to the first Duke of Buckingham, George Villiers (1592-1628) who acquired the estate at Helmsley through marriage to the Earl of Rutland's daughter in 1620. "Bald" or "Ball'd" at the time possibly meant a round, bulging forehead, with a white marking, which possibly extended as far down as the lips, supporting Old Bald Peg being an Arabian, although her origins are uncertain, different sources describing her as an Arabian and a Barb.
Dam of Old Morocco Mare through which the maternal line of the family continues and also the dam of Spanker, important sire and described as the best racehorse at Newmarket in the time of Charles II; Charming Jenny (dam of Betty Percival, Highland Laddie, Hampton Court Charming Jenny and Leedes); and Young Bald Peg (grandam of top class racehorse and twice Champion Sire Fox)

Owner: 1) Thomas Wharton, Earl of Wharton 2) Edward Leedes
SubjectInfoText:
Owner: 1) Thomas Wharton, Earl of Wharton 2) Edward Leedes
Breeder: Edward Leedes
11 May 1698 - beaten 500 guineas match Newmarket versus His Majesty's [King William III] Stiff Dick, who carries a featherweight compared to 9 stone by Careless (this was Careless' only career defeat); April 1699 - won £1,900 match Newmarket versus an unknown opponent owned by the Duke of Devonshire
Contemporary accounts record that eventually he "ceased to run at Newmarket merely for lack of competition".
When he was 14 years old Lord Wharton refused an offer of £700 for Careless and presented him to his breeder Edward Leedes. Careless stood at Leedes' stud in Yorkshire.
Sire of Betty Leedes, dam of Flying Childers (outstanding racehorse and Champion Sire 1730 and 1736) and Bartlet's Childers (Champion Sire 1742); an unnamed mare which is grandam of Vixen (1742 King's Plate [mares] Hambleton, 1743 King's Plate [mares] Newmarket); and an unnamed mare which is dam of Hobgoblin (good class winner between 1729 and 1732)

Owner: 1) John Manners, 1st Duke of Rutland 2) John Manners, 2nd Duke of Rutland 3) Henry Carter
SubjectInfoText:
Owner: 1) John Manners, 1st Duke of Rutland 2) John Manners, 2nd Duke of Rutland 3) Henry Carter
First owned by John Manners, 1st Duke of Rutland and, on his death in 1711, probably by John Manners, the 2nd Duke, Rutland's Grey Turk was advertised for sale in 1714-15 by his subsequent owner, Henry Carter, at the "Sign of the Faulcon near Enfield, in Staffordshire". He may have been at stud in Yorkshire prior to this.
Sire of the dam of Easby Snake [Ellerker's Snake], sire of 1) Mother Western, grandam of Eclipse, greatest racehorse of 18th century and progenitor of Darley Arabian male line to over 95% of today's thoroughbreds; 2) Miss Hendry (1735 King's Plate [mares] Hambleton; and 3) Orion, sire of the grandam of Alipes (1761 Jockey Club Plate, Great Subscription Purse [4yo division] York)

Owner: 1) Matthew Lister 2) Mr. Robinson
Breeder: Matthew Lister
"He was never in training, being rendered incapable by a tumourous swelling that attended him in his youth; and from a suppostion that the same was the effect of the sting or bite of some venomous reptile, was therefore called Snake" [Pick 1:6]
Sire of Bay Wilkinson (1724 King's Plate Newmarket); the very useful racehorse [Beaver's] Driver; Lowther (1731 King's Plate Nottingham); Easby Snake (sire of Mother Western, grandam of Eclipse, the greatest racehorse of the 18th century and a great sire); [William's] Squirrel (1725 King's Plates York, Newmarket, 1726 King's Plate Newmarket);
the dam of Thunderbolt (1729 King's Plates Winchester, Lewes, Canterbury, Newmarket); the dam of Poor Robin (1741 King's Plates Salisbury, Winchester, Lewes) and of very useful racehorse Arthur O'Bradley; the grandam of [Old] Standard (1742 King's Plate Guildford, sire of Jason, winner of 7 King's Plates); dam of Stump (winner of 3 King's Plates); and 3rd dam of Sampson (winner of 6 King's Plates)

Owner: 1) Monsieur St. Victor 2) Captain William Rider
SubjectInfoText:
Owner: 1) Monsieur St. Victor 2) Captain William Rider
Purchased from a Monsieur St. Victor (possibly the same man who helped King James II's queen and infant son escape to France in 1688), he becomes a stallion in the stud of Captain William Rider, a warden of Whittlebury Forest, Northamptonshire, where his influence is extensive despite his limited known progeny
Sire of 1) Bald Galloway (Champion Sire 1725, sire of a) Grey Robinson, dam of Regulus (1745 King's Plates Winchester, Salisbury, Nottingham, Canterbury, Lewes, Lincoln, Newmarket, 1746 King's Plate Newmarket, undefeated in 9 starts and Champion Sire 1754, 1755, 1756, 1757, 1761, 1763, 1765 and 1766);
b) Roxana (dam of Cade - Champion Sire 1752, 1753, 1758, 1759 and 1760, sire of Matchem; of Lath (undefeated in 4 starts); and of Roundhead, sire of high class racehorses Stadtholder and Joseph Andrews; and sire of 2) Points (grandam of the outstanding broodmare Amorett and of the excellent broodmare Sachrissa); and of 3) Rider's Cupid (which sires a couple of mares which breed on)
